do.narrate: Mel Function's home office: three monitors, one career, and a mug population that has achieved self-governance. Somewhere out there, the entire internet just stopped working. In here, it smells like yesterday's coffee. Mostly because of all the yesterdays.
Three monitors, all frozen mid-scroll. The dashboard's last recorded status is a wall of red so uniform it looks like modern art. Titled, presumably, 'Everything (2026).'

look.text: Still red. Refreshing it with your mind hasn't worked the last forty times either.

use.text: You jiggle the mouse with the confidence of a professional. The dashboards remain a monument to the color red.

look.text: Your field uniform. It has seen four thousand video calls and zero physical meetings, which is exactly how everyone prefers it.

do.narrate: You claim the hoodie. Somewhere in the distance, a dress code weeps.

take.text: You're already carrying it. One hoodie is a wardrobe; two is a cry for help.

look.text: Your homelab: the only infrastructure within five hundred miles that still works, because you are the only person within five hundred miles who reads changelogs.

use.text: You check the rack. Green lights, all of them. It's the loveliest thing you've seen all day, and you saw it eleven minutes ago too.

take.text: It's bolted, racked, and cable-managed. Some relationships are permanent.

look.text: Main Street, population: dwindling. Half the storefronts are for lease and the other half sell candles. Today it looks the same as always, which is impressive for the first day of the apocalypse.

open.text: You crack the window. Fresh air enters, panics, and leaves.

look.text: A museum of caffeine spanning several eras of your career. Some of these mugs contain civilizations now. You've been meaning to grant them independence.

do.narrate: You select the least biohazardous mug. It's coming with you. For morale.

look.text: Ergonomic, allegedly. It has molded itself so precisely to your shape that sitting anywhere else now feels like a betrayal on both sides.

use.text: Tempting. But the internet isn't going to fix itself. You checked. Repeatedly.
